input.background: A laboratory kit states that the standard deviation of its results can be expected to vary with σ=1 unit. A lab technician takes four measurements using this kit.
input.question: How many times must we repeat the measurement before we obtain a standard deviation of the mean measurement of 0.2 units?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: SE_x = σ/√n. Therefore, n = (σ/SE_x̄)^2. To achieve a standard error of 0.2, use n = (1/0.2)^2 = 25.

sigma.description: The standard deviation of the laboratory kit results.

desired_standard_deviation.description: The target standard deviation of the mean measurement.

n.description: The number of measurements needed to achieve the desired standard deviation of the mean.
